channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: US-based Original Six Red Wings, Rangers, Bruins, Blackhawks eliminated from playoffs for 1st time
item.description: The Original Six teams based in the United States have a rich history, combining to hoist the Stanley Cup 27 times.
item.dc:creator: Larry Lage, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters playoff to complete the career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Rory McIlroy turned another major collapse into his grandest moment of all, hitting a wedge into 3 feet for birdie in a sudden-death playoff Sunday to finally win the Masters and take his place in golf history as the sixth player to claim the career Grand Slam.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Justin Rose played a memorable round at the Masters. It wasn't enough to deny Rory McIlroy the title
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Justin Rose kept hitting balls to stay loose at the tournament practice facility, all alone save for longtime caddie Mark Fulcher, everything eerily quiet around him while drama was unfolding elsewhere on Sunday at the Masters.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Report: Bears sign Kyler Gordon to record extension, make him highest-paid slot corner in NFL
item.description: The Chicago Bears have agreed to a three-year, $40 million contract extension with corner Kyler Gordon, making him one of the highest-paid corners in the NFL and keeping him in Chicago for at least the next four seasons.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: NBA's West spots set, with the Nuggets, Clippers and Timberwolves in and the Warriors play-in bound
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers worked overtime to clinch a playoff spot — and send the Golden State Warriors to the NBA's play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palou finally loses as IndyCar champ's 2-race winning streak snapped by Kirkwood in Long Beach
item.description: Kyle Kirkwood showed that Alex Palou can be beat this IndyCar season and returned Andretti Global to the top of the podium with his second career victory at the Grand Prix of Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Warriors' playoff seeding set after loss against Clippers in regular-season finale
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors' playoff path is set. They will play in the NBA Play-In Tournament following a home overtime loss to the Los Angeles Clippers in Sunday's regular-season finale. With the loss, the Warriors will face the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Kyle Larson dominates and claims an emotional Cup victory at Bristol Motor Speedway
item.description: Kyle Larson led 411 of 500 laps and scored an emotional win on Sunday with a dominating NASCAR Cup Series victory at Bristol Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Nate Ryan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Report: Cubs' Justin Steele to undergo elbow surgery, done for the season
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The Chicago Cubs were dealt a heavy blow on the injury front Sunday evening. All-Star starting pitcher Justin Steele is done for the season, according to the Chicago Sun-Times.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: In Year 20, the Spurs' Chris Paul starts all 82 games. And he made some history in the process
item.description: In Year 20, Chris Paul started all 82. And he made some NBA history in the process. Paul was in the San Antonio Spurs' starting lineup for their season finale on Sunday, making him the first player to ever make 82 starts this deep into a career.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Garrett Crochet dazzles in return to South Side, takes no-no into the 8th in Red Sox victory
item.description: Garrett Crochet returned to the South Side with a vengeance, throwing seven and one-third innings and striking out eleven, but the Red Sox won the game 3-1 to avoid a sweep.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Pacers rally from 23-point deficit in 4th quarter to defeat Cavaliers 126-118 in double overtime
item.description: Quenton Jackson scored 21 points, RayJ Dennis had seven in the second overtime and the Indiana Pacers rallied for a 126-118 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers after trailing by 23 points with 7:35 remaining in regulation.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Celtics star Jaylen Brown received injections in right knee, coach Joe Mazzulla says
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Boston Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla on Sunday confirmed a report that Jaylen Brown received injections during the week to help his troublesome right knee that’s limited his playing time the past month. It was first reported by ESPN that Brown received pain management injections.
item.dc:creator: KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dave Portnoy waves Israel flag in celebration after Holocaust denier Bryce Mitchell gets choked out at UFC 314
item.description: Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy celebrated Bryce Mitchell, who has publicly praised Adolf Hitler, getting choked out by Jean Silva at UFC 314 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Payton Pritchard scores 34 points in Celtics' 93-86 regular-season ending victory over Hornets
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Payton Pritchard scored 34 points on Sunday and the Boston Celtics swept consecutive home games against the Charlotte Hornets with a 93-86 victory despite blowing a 21-point first-half lead in a regular-season ending matchup of all reserves.
item.dc:creator: KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steph Curry expected to play in Warriors’ regular season finale vs. Clippers
item.description: Steph Curry injured his thumb against Portland, Kerr believes he will play vs. Clippers with playoff and play-in seeding on the line.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Dycus, The Mercury News

item.title: US downs defending champion Canada 2-1 to stay perfect at women’s hockey worlds
item.description: The United States downed defending champion Canada 2-1 at the women’s hockey world championship in a matchup of the game’s global powers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cubs have big night in 16-0 rout of Dodgers, handing LA the worst home shutout loss in franchise history
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers franchise has been around since 1884, going back to its days in Brooklyn. And no team had ever done to the Dodgers in front of their home crowd what the Cubs did at Dodger Stadium on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: MIKE DIGIOVANNA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Rowdies lose homecoming matchup, but stadium comeback brings hope
item.description: For the first time in seven months, Rowdies fans are finally back home at Al Lang Stadium after the waterfront venue was impacted during back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rory McIlroy has masterful start at Augusta and closes in on career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The greatest start in the 91-year history of the Masters wasn't enough for Rory McIlroy. He delivered what looked to be a haymaker late Saturday afternoon, a 6-iron so majestic and pure that he walked 10 yards before he saw it land 6 feet away for eagle on the 15th hole.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Patrick Reed and Ludvig Aberg make Saturday moves at the Masters. Justin Rose drops back
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — Patrick Reed says his putter has been “on vacation.” Golf fans who primarily watch major championships and the PGA Tour might have the impression that Reed has been lounging on a beach somewhere, too.
item.dc:creator: BEN NUCKOLS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Daly rakes in exorbitant amount of money during Masters week, thanks to annual Hooters appearance
item.description: John Daly's team told ESPN he made $780,000 at his annual Hooters' appearance during Masters week last year, as the legendary golfer is a hit among fans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Taylor gets 3 hits as the White Sox stop 8-game slide by routing the Red Sox 11-1
item.description: Michael A. Taylor had three hits and scored three times, and the Chicago White Sox stopped an eight-game slide by routing the sloppy Boston Red Sox 11-1 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Local golf club students attend The Masters for first time
item.description: It was a beautiful Friday for the second round of The Masters, and some middle schoolers got to attend the tournament for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Brad Means, Fox 5

item.title: Blackhawks host the Jets following Foligno's 2-goal game
item.description: Winnipeg Jets (54-21-4, in the Central Division) vs. Chicago Blackhawks (23-46-10, in the Central Division) Chicago; Saturday, 7 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: The Chicago Blackhawks host the Winnipeg Jets after Nick Foligno's two-goal game against the Boston Bruins in the Blackhawks' 5-2 win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Dodgers look to keep home win streak going, host the Cubs
item.description: Chicago Cubs (9-6, first in the NL Central) vs. Los Angeles Dodgers (10-4, third in the NL West) Los Angeles; Friday, 10:10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tampa Bay Rowdies dismiss head coach ahead of home opener
item.description: A spokesperson for the Tampa Bay Rowdies announced Friday that the club has relieved head coach Robbie Neilson of his duties.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: BYU lands point guard Robert Wright III from Baylor
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It didn't take long for Kevin Young to find his new point guard. According to multiple reports, Baylor's Robert Wright III is transferring to BYU for his sophomore season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: College athletes and San Diego students with disabilities team up for day of sports
item.description: The Inclusive Sports Foundation has provided children with special needs the opportunity to play sports with college athletes and recently stopped by the University of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Julian Del Gaudio, Fox 5

item.title: Northwestern University to settle lawsuit over football program hazing allegations
item.description: Northwestern University has agreed to settle a lawsuit filed by students against the school and former head coach Pat Fitzgerald, who alleged hazing incidents took place in the football program, but the settlement does not resolve Fitzgerald's lawsuit against the university.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9
